title: Idempotency and Repeat-Run Discipline
notes: Idempotency empirically verified: re-running pytest produce identical pass/fail counts. Run artifacts (UUID-based) are unique per run. Append-only event logs verified by validate_events_append_only(). Source files unchanged by re-run.

checks.evidence: All phase output dirs populated with expected files

checks.check: Second run does not duplicate non-repeatable artifacts
checks.evidence: Run IDs use UUIDs; re-runs create new entries, never overwrite

checks.check: Append-only logs append without modifying prior entries
checks.evidence: validate_events_append_only() confirms no duplicate IDs, no truncation
PX-RYYY, PX-CYYY, PX-TYYY, PX-GYYY, PX-BYYY, PX-FYYY IDs are static
Re-running `make test-quick` and `make test-release` produces same pass/fail with no source changes

checks.check: Generated evidence has new run IDs only where appropriate
checks.evidence: Pipeline runs produce unique run_<uuid> dirs

checks.check: Repeated benchmark runs produce equivalent results
checks.evidence: Re-running test_governance_benchmarks.py produces same PASS set

checks.check: Rerun after failure does not hide the original failure artifact
checks.evidence: Failure artifacts in phase_5_failure_recovery/ are immutable

checks.check: Rerun after rollback confirms source state remains clean
checks.evidence: Rollback tests verify source state restored after failure

checks.check: Final acceptance reports identify which run they summarize
checks.evidence: All reports have timestamp_utc field identifying their run
